home.title: Virtual Medical Laboratory
home.tagline: Choose a lab. Complete each experiment step by step — practise or take the exam.
home.footer: {done} of 6 labs ready · the rest are coming soon

1.title: Bacterial cell morphology
1.subtitle: Simple stain — methylene blue
1.desc: Prepare a smear, fix it and stain with methylene blue to study the cell shape under the microscope.

2.title: Gram staining method
2.subtitle: Differential staining
2.desc: Use crystal violet, Lugol, alcohol and fuchsin to separate Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.

3.title: Drigalski plating
3.subtitle: Spreader inoculation — pure culture
3.desc: Spread the suspension across 3 plates in turn to isolate single colonies and obtain a pure culture, then Gram stain.

4.title: Antibiotic sensitivity
4.subtitle: Paper-disk (disk-diffusion) method
4.desc: Lawn-inoculate the culture, place antibiotic disks, measure the zones of inhibition and determine sensitivity.

5.title: Studying living microorganisms
5.subtitle: «Crushed-drop» preparation
5.desc: Prepare a crushed-drop mount of living bacteria in saline, cover it with a cover slip and observe motility at ×40.

6.title: Ziehl-Neelsen method
6.subtitle: Acid-fast staining
6.desc: Detect the tuberculosis bacillus (AFB) with carbol fuchsin, acid and methylene blue.
